finding: Slovak firms shifted toward fixed investment while working capital needs stayed dominant
bullets: Fixed investment financing rose from 34.2% to 40.7% among Slovak firms (n=242), a 6.5 pp increase that lifted SK above the EA average of 38.4% — suggesting stronger growth-oriented investment.

Inventory and working capital remained the most-cited purpose at 57.0% (n=242), 16.2 pp above the EA average of 40.8%, continuing a persistent structural gap — highlighting ongoing liquidity constraints.
Refinancing or paying off obligations declined by 4.5 pp to 17.0% (n=241), converging toward the EA rate of 15.8% — indicating reduced debt-servicing pressure.
chart_subtitle: Slovak fixed investment citations rose to 40.7% in wave 39.
